accept: Unknown subcommand returns: 'act: unknown subcommand "<arg>"; run "act help" for the list' (exit 2).
Bare 'act dep' (no verb) returns a usage line listing 'add' (and any other future verbs), not 'not implemented yet'.
'act dep --help' (or any flag-like first token after dep) routes to the dep-level usage rather than reusing the unknown-verb fallback.
Tests cover: act <typo>, act dep, act dep <typo>, act dep --help, act dep -h.
payload.description: Discovered 2026-05-10 by UX-eval agent. 'act dep --help' returns 'act dep --help: not implemented yet'. 'act <typo>' returns 'act <typo>: not implemented yet'. Same message for two very different conditions: dep IS implemented (as 'dep add'), and an unknown subcommand is just unknown. The eval agent reported being misled into thinking dep itself was a stub.
payload.title: act dep --help and unknown subcommands share misleading error
